# Beudox HR - Technical Context for AI Assistants

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a single-page React application built with TypeScript and Vite. It follows a component-based architecture with clear separation of concerns:

- **Frontend-only application** with Supabase as the backend service
- **Component-driven development** using shadcn/ui for consistent UI
- **Query-based data fetching** with TanStack Query for caching and synchronization
- **Route-based navigation** using React Router

## Key Technologies & Patterns

### Core Framework
- React 18 with functional components and hooks
- TypeScript for type safety
- Vite for fast development and building

### UI & Styling
- Tailwind CSS for utility-first styling
- shadcn/ui component library for consistent design system
- Lucide React for icons
- Custom CSS variables for theming

### Data Management
- Supabase client for database operations and authentication
- TanStack Query for server state management
- Real-time subscriptions for live updates

### Rich Text Editing
- Tiptap editor with extensions (StarterKit, Underline, Link)
- HTML-based content storage

### Routing & Navigation
- React Router with custom NavLink wrapper
- Programmatic navigation with useNavigate

## Database Schema (Supabase)

The application uses PostgreSQL via Supabase with the following main entities:

- **employees**: User profiles with roles, departments, contact info
- **leave_requests**: Leave applications with approval workflow
- **evaluations**: Quarterly performance reviews
- **daily_evaluations**: Daily feedback system
- **hr_policies**: Company policies and documents
- **departments**: Organizational structure
- **roles**: Permission-based access control
- **expense_categories**: Expense tracking categories
- **notifications**: In-app notification system

## Authentication & Authorization

- Supabase Auth for user authentication
- Role-based access control (CEO, HR Manager, Team Lead, Employee)
- Permission checks in components and API calls
- Session management with React context

## Component Architecture

### Layout Components
- `AppLayout`: Main application wrapper with sidebar navigation
- `AppSidebar`: Collapsible navigation menu
- `TopBar`: Header with notifications and user menu
- `NotificationBell`: Real-time notification indicator

### Feature Components
- **Leave Management**: Request forms, approval modals, balance tracking
- **Evaluations**: Timeline views, quarterly/daily evaluation forms
- **Settings**: Tabbed interface for various configuration options
- **Employee Selection**: Searchable dropdown with avatars

### UI Components
- Comprehensive shadcn/ui component library
- Custom components like `RichTextEditor`, `SearchableEmployeeSelect`
- Consistent theming with CSS custom properties

## Key Files & Their Purposes

### Entrypoints
- `src/main.tsx`: Application bootstrap, renders App component
- `src/pages/Index.tsx`: Root page component (dashboard)

### Core Logic
- `src/App.tsx`: Main app component with routing
- `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`: Supabase client configuration
- `src/hooks/useAuth.ts`: Authentication state management
- `src/lib/utils.ts`: Utility functions (cn for class merging)
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Permission checking logic

### Business Logic
- `src/lib/leave-utils.ts`: Leave calculation and validation
- `src/lib/notifications.ts`: Notification sending logic
- `src/lib/format-role.ts`: Role formatting utilities

### Components
- `src/components/BeudoxLogo.tsx`: Logo component with variant support
- `src/components/NavLink.tsx`: React Router NavLink wrapper
- `src/components/SearchableEmployeeSelect.tsx`: Employee selection dropdown
- `src/components/evaluations/EvaluationTimeline.tsx`: Evaluation history display
- `src/components/hr-policies/RichTextEditor.tsx`: Rich text editing component

## Configuration Files

- `vite.config.ts`: Vite build configuration
- `tailwind.config.ts`: Tailwind CSS configuration
- `tsconfig.json`: TypeScript configuration
- `supabase/config.toml`: Supabase project configuration
- `playwright.config.ts`: End-to-end testing configuration

## Development Workflow

1. **Local Development**: `npm run dev` starts Vite dev server
2. **Database Changes**: Migrations in `supabase/migrations/` applied via Supabase CLI
3. **Testing**: Unit tests with Vitest, E2E with Playwright
4. **Build**: `npm run build` creates production bundle

## API Integration Patterns

- Direct Supabase client usage for CRUD operations
- Query keys following TanStack Query conventions
- Error handling with try/catch and user feedback
- Optimistic updates for better UX
- Real-time subscriptions for live data

## Security Considerations

- Environment variables for sensitive configuration
- Row Level Security (RLS) policies in Supabase
- Client-side validation with server-side enforcement
- Role-based UI rendering and API access

## Performance Optimizations

- Component lazy loading where appropriate
- Query caching and background refetching
- Image optimization with proper sizing
- Bundle splitting with Vite
- Efficient re-renders with React.memo and useMemo

## Deployment

- Built as static files deployable to any CDN
- Environment-specific configuration
- Supabase handles backend scaling
- CI/CD pipeline for automated testing and deployment
